summary: TSLA CSP best setup: oversold + decent premium
NVDA LEAP attractive: IV cheap vs history
GOOG CSP viable: elevated IV for premium
body:
═══════════════════════════════════

• Capital: Enough for 1 CSP (100 shares)

• LEAP thesis: Long-term bullish on AI/earnings growth
• IV Rank proxy: IV/HV1y ratio (Barchart unavailable)
  — Ratio >1.2 = elevated IV, <0.8 = cheap IV

IV Environment: ELEVATED

Momentum: Daily RSI 60.3 is neutral territory. Weekly RSI 58.0 neutral.

Decision Flowchart:
  Step 1 (IV): Elevated → premium selling has edge ✅
  Step 2 (Conviction): Moderate → CSP appropriate, not aggressive LEAP
  Step 3 (Capital): $29K+ needed for CSP at ~$290 strike

  ➤ RECOMMENDATION: Sell a Cash-Secured Put
    IV is elevated, giving you a premium edge. Moderate conviction
    means a CSP is the right vehicle — you'd be happy owning at
    a discount. LEAP not ideal when IV is rich (you'd overpay).
    CC viable if you already hold shares.

    CSP premium will be thin since IV is low. If you believe in
    NVDA's AI growth story, a LEAP at these IV levels is attractive.
    However, moderate conviction = smaller position size.

    TSLA is oversold on both timeframes — potential bounce territory.
    IV is decent for premium collection. A CSP at support lets you
    either collect premium on the bounce or get assigned at a great
    price. This is the best risk/reward setup of the three tickers.
    Note: IV isn't elevated, so also consider a small LEAP position
    to capture the potential rebound with leverage.
